# @genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui

Shared **design tokens**, a **Tailwind CSS preset**, **global styles** (including fonts), and **React marketing primitives** for Genius Sports microsites and landing experiences.

This package is meant to sit **alongside** consumer apps: you keep product-specific sections, copy, and data in the app; you pull **visual language, layout shells, and reusable UI** from here.

## What you get

| Area | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Tokens** | TypeScript constants (`colors`, `spacingScale`, `containerWidthsPx`, typography, radii, shadows, breakpoints) plus a mirrored **`tokens.css`** file of CSS custom properties for non-Tailwind contexts. |
| **Tailwind** | A **`tailwind-preset`** that extends theme with Genius fonts, colors, container max-widths, shadows, radii, and semantic color aliases wired to CSS variables. |
| **Global CSS** | Base layer rules: box sizing, smooth scroll, font smoothing, page background, body/`#root` styling, heading font family defaults. Pulls in **`@font-face`** and token variables. |
| **React UI** | Buttons, cards, chips, typography helpers, modal, section shell, accordion motion helpers, page layout wrapper, and a small **`cn`**-style utility pattern inside components (the **`cn` helper is not a public export**). |
| **Assets** | Fonts (Klarheit trial, Red Hat Text) and brand SVGs copied into **`dist/assets/`** at build time for apps to import via the **`./assets/*`** export. |

---

## Requirements

**Runtime / tooling (consumer apps)**

- A modern bundler (Vite, webpack 5, etc.) that understands ESM **`import`** and can process CSS.
- **Tailwind CSS 3.4.x** configured to scan this package’s built files (see [Tailwind setup](#tailwind-setup)).

**Peer dependencies** (must be installed in the consuming app; versions should satisfy the ranges below):

| Package | Range | Role |
|---------|--------|------|
| `react` | ^19 | Components are React 19–oriented. |
| `react-dom` | ^19 | DOM rendering. |
| `tailwindcss` | ^3.4 | Preset targets Tailwind 3.4 APIs. |
| `framer-motion` | ^12 | Used by `Modal`, `AccordionMotionPanel`, and related motion. |

The library build marks `react`, `react-dom`, `framer-motion`, and `tailwindcss` as **external** so they are not bundled into `dist/`; your app supplies them.

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install @genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui
```

Install peers in the same project (adjust versions to match your app’s constraints):

```bash
npm install react react-dom tailwindcss framer-motion
```

---

## Quick start

**1. Tailwind** — use the preset and include the package’s built files in `content`:

```ts
// tailwind.config.ts
import type { Config } from 'tailwindcss'
import gsMarketingUiPreset from '@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/tailwind-preset'

export default {
  presets: [gsMarketingUiPreset],
  content: [
    './index.html',
    './src/**/*.{ts,tsx}',
    './node_modules/@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/dist/**/*.{js,mjs}',
  ],
} satisfies Config
```

**2. Global CSS** — import package styles **before** your `@tailwind` directives so layers and variables resolve correctly:

```css
@import '@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles';
@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;
```

**3. Components** — import from the package root:

```tsx
import {
  Button,
  Card,
  Chip,
  Heading,
  Text,
  Modal,
  SectionShell,
  MarketingPageLayout,
} from '@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui'
```

Keep **only** app-specific `theme.extend` entries in the app’s Tailwind config; shared marketing tokens belong in this preset so microsites stay visually aligned.

---

## Package exports

| Subpath | Points to | Use for |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| `@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui` | `./dist/index.js` + types | React components, layouts, and token modules. |
| `@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles` | `./dist/styles/index.css` | Single entry for global base styles + fonts + CSS variables. |
| `@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles/index.css` | same | Explicit path alias for the same file. |
| `@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/tailwind-preset` | `./dist/tailwind-preset.js` | `presets: [gsMarketingUiPreset]` in `tailwind.config`. |
| `@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/assets/*` | `./dist/assets/*` | Fonts, logos, motifs (`?url` or similar, depending on bundler). |

The npm **`files`** field publishes **`dist/`** only. CSS under `dist/styles/` and `dist/tokens/tokens.css` is produced by the build’s static copy step (see [Developing locally](#developing-locally)).

Because **`sideEffects`** includes `**/*.css`, bundlers should **not** tree-shake imported stylesheets away.

---

## Tailwind setup

1. Add the **preset** as shown in [Quick start](#quick-start).
2. Extend **`content`** so Tailwind sees class names inside **`node_modules/@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/dist/**/*.{js,mjs}`**. Without this, utilities used only inside the package will be purged and styles will look broken.
3. Prefer **preset-driven** tokens for new work; use the app’s `theme.extend` for **one-off** app branding only.

---

## Global CSS and import order

`@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles` resolves to `dist/styles/index.css`, which:

- Imports **`fonts.css`** (`@font-face` rules; URLs are relative to that file).
- Imports **`tokens.css`** (CSS custom properties under `:root`).
- Defines an **`@layer base`** block for `html`, `body`, `#root`, and heading font families.

**Import this stylesheet before `@tailwind base`** so your app’s Tailwind layers can build on the same variables and font setup. Bundlers such as Vite rewrite `url()` references when processing the entry CSS so font files end up in the app’s build output.

---

## Design tokens

Tokens exist in two forms that **must stay in sync**:

1. **TypeScript** (`src/tokens/*.ts`) — imported by the Tailwind preset and re-exported from the package root for use in JS/TS (charts, inline styles, tests).
2. **`tokens.css`** — `:root` custom properties for raw CSS, third-party widgets, or app code that prefers variables.

When you change hex values or add semantic colors, update **both** the TS sources and `src/tokens/tokens.css`. See **[`src/tokens/MIGRATION.md`](src/tokens/MIGRATION.md)** for accent migration, deprecated typography keys, layout notes, accordion/modal tokens, and other migration guidance.

---

## Tailwind preset reference

The preset extends Tailwind’s theme with Genius marketing defaults. Highlights:

| Extension | Notes |
|-----------|--------|
| **`screens`** | `sm`–`2xl` at 640px–1536px (standard Tailwind breakpoints). |
| **`maxWidth`** | `max-w-container-narrow` (960px), `max-w-container-default` (1200px), `max-w-container-wide` (1440px). |
| **`fontFamily`** | `font-heading` (Klarheit trial stack), `font-body` (Red Hat Text stack). |
| **`fontSize`** | Brand headings `text-brand-h1`–`text-brand-h4`, body `text-body` / `text-body-sm`, section `text-section-title` / `text-section-copy`, leads `text-lead`, `text-lead-lg`, `text-lead-xl`. |
| **`fontWeight`** | `light`, `normal`, `medium`, `semibold`, `bold`. |
| **`colors`** | Palette literals (`navy`, `blue`, `text`, `border`, `gs-*`, etc.) plus semantic entries bound to CSS vars: `gs-bg`, `gs-surface`, `foreground`, `muted`, `surface`, `accent`, `accent-hover`. |
| **`borderRadius`** | `rounded-brand`, `rounded-pill`. |
| **`boxShadow`** | `shadow-soft`, `shadow-card`, `shadow-card-hover`, `shadow-sequence`, `shadow-handle`, `shadow-modal-panel`. |

Spacing scale and container widths also exist as **CSS variables** in `tokens.css` (e.g. `--space-1`…`--space-6`, `--container-*`).

---

## Components and layouts

All public components are **forwardRef**-friendly where it matters (e.g. `Button`, `Section`) and accept optional **`className`** for composition.

### Layout

| Export | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **`MarketingPageLayout`** | Full-height page wrapper with `min-h-screen` and `bg-gs-bg`. |
| **`Section`** | Thin `forwardRef` wrapper around `<section>` for semantic regions. |
| **`SectionShell`** | Centered content column with responsive horizontal padding (`px-6 lg:px-12`) and configurable max width: `narrow` \| `default` \| `wide` → `max-w-container-*`. |

### Typography

| Export | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **`Heading`** | `level`: `h1` \| `h2` \| `h3` \| `h4` — applies brand heading scale and `font-heading`. |
| **`Text`** | `variant`: `body` \| `bodySm` \| `lead` \| `leadLg` \| `leadXl` \| `muted`; optional `as`: `p` \| `span`. |

### Actions and surfaces

| Export | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **`Button`** | **`variant`** (required): `primaryPill`, `secondaryPill`, `solidAccent`, `ghost`, `outlineOnDark`. Primary/secondary pills use **`GeniusStripHoverBg`** animation. Standard button `type` defaults to `"button"`. |
| **`Card`** | **`variant`**: `brand` (light grey surface + hover lift), `feature` (white bordered), `plain` (transparent bordered). |
| **`Chip`** | Small pill label with border and muted text. |

### Overlays and disclosure

| Export | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **`Modal`** | Controlled dialog: `open`, `onClose`, `titleId`, `title`, `children`. Optional `size`: `md` \| `xl` (max width), `closeLabel`, `panelClassName`, `bodyClassName`. Locks body scroll, **Escape** closes, uses Framer Motion + reduced-motion handling. |
| **`AccordionMotionPanel`** | Height/opacity animation for disclosure content; parent controls `open` and supplies `panelId` for `aria-controls`. |
| **`AccordionToggleIcon`** | Visual toggle affordance for accordions. |
| **`accordionTriggerFocusClass`** | Shared focus ring class string for disclosure triggers. |
| **`GeniusStripHoverBg`** | Animated strip used inside pill buttons. |

### Tokens (re-exported)

Import named values from the package root, e.g. `colors`, `tailwindColorLiterals`, `fontFamilies`, `fontSizes`, `screens`, `radius`, `boxShadows`, `spacingScale`, `containerWidthsPx`, etc. Use these when you need the same numbers in TypeScript as in CSS/Tailwind.

---

## Fonts and static assets

Source assets live under **`src/assets/`**. Each **`npm run build`** copies them to **`dist/assets/`**, which is what consumers resolve through **`@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/assets/*`**.

| Location (in published package) | Contents |
|---------------------------------|----------|
| `dist/assets/fonts/` | Klarheit (trial woff2 plus full otf/ttf where applicable) and Red Hat Text woff2. |
| `dist/assets/logos/` | Genius wordmark and G mark (SVG). |
| `dist/assets/motifs/` | Shared decorative imagery (e.g. line treatments). |

**Vite example** — reference a logo URL:

```ts
import geniusLogoUrl from '@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/assets/logos/genius_logo.svg?url'
```

`@font-face` rules live in the built **`dist/styles/fonts.css`** and use paths relative to that file (e.g. `../assets/fonts/...`). Importing **`@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles`** from your app ensures the bundler can trace and emit those font files.

Apps may keep **`public/fonts`** or other static paths during migration; this package does not require removing them.

---

## Motion and accessibility

- **Framer Motion** drives enter/exit and height animations in **`Modal`** and **`AccordionMotionPanel`**.
- **`useReducedMotionSafe`** wraps Framer’s `useReducedMotion()` so `null` on first paint does not break boolean logic.
- **`Modal`** sets `document.body.style.overflow` while open and restores it on teardown; it listens for **Escape**. Ensure **`titleId`** matches the visible title element’s `id` for **`aria-labelledby`** (see component implementation in `src/components/Modal.tsx`).
- For accordion patterns, pair **`AccordionMotionPanel`** with a button that toggles **`open`** and references **`panelId`** via **`aria-controls`** / **`aria-expanded`**.

---

## Building and publishing this package

- **`npm run build`** — `tsup` emits ESM **`dist/index.js`**, **`dist/tailwind-preset.js`**, source maps, and **`.d.ts`** files; **`scripts/copy-dist-static.mjs`** then copies `src/styles/**`, `src/tokens/tokens.css`, and `src/assets/**` into **`dist/`**.
- **`prepare`** runs **`npm run build`** after install in a git checkout (useful for consumers installing from git).
- **`prepublishOnly`** runs **`npm run build`** before **`npm publish`** so the tarball always contains a fresh **`dist/`**.

Published tarballs include **only** the **`files`** list (`dist`), per `package.json`.

---

## Developing locally

| Command | What it does |
|---------|----------------|
| **`npm run build`** | One-off production build + static asset copy. |
| **`npm run dev`** | `tsup --watch`; after each successful build, the same **`onSuccess`** copy step refreshes `dist/styles`, `dist/tokens`, and `dist/assets`. |

**Typecheck:** this repo uses TypeScript in **`noEmit`** mode via `tsconfig.json` for editor and `tsup` dts generation; run **`npx tsc --noEmit`** if you want a standalone typecheck from the CLI.

To test the package inside another app without publishing, use **`npm pack`**, **`file:`** / **`workspace:`** dependencies, or **`npm link`**, then verify Tailwind **`content`** includes this package’s **`dist`** paths.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
|---------|----------------|-----|
| Utilities from the package have no effect | Tailwind purged unused classes | Add `./node_modules/@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/dist/**/*.{js,mjs}` to **`content`**. |
| Fonts not loading | Styles not imported, or wrong import order | Import **`@genius-sports/gs-marketing-ui/styles`** before **`@tailwind`** in your entry CSS. |
| Broken layout / variables undefined | Skipped global stylesheet | Ensure the app actually imports the **`styles`** export (not only the preset). |
| Peer dependency warnings | Version skew | Align `react`, `react-dom`, `tailwindcss`, and `framer-motion` with the peer ranges in this package’s `package.json`. |
| Stale `dist` after git pull | Prepare/build not run | Run **`npm run build`** in the package (or reinstall so **`prepare`** runs). |
